reasoning: First, I identified the reference class as "pricing for flagship first-party Nintendo games on the Switch 2 generation." Since a mainline 3D Mario title is undeniably one of Nintendo's most premium, high-budget, system-selling franchises—sharing top billing with Zelda and Super Smash Bros.—it is virtually impossible that it would be priced lower than the established new baseline of $70. The only marginal uncertainty (around 3-5%) accounts for completely unanticipated possibilities, such as a smaller experimental project mistakenly billed as 'mainline' or extreme business shifts.
